outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Hegseth, Caine encounter intense bipartisan frustration as Iran war cost hits $29 billion
outlets.summary: WaPo reports on bipartisan congressional frustration at Hegseth's Iran war testimony, with Pentagon confirming $29 billion war cost (primarily munitions replacement). McConnell criticized Hegseth over NATO strains. Hegseth defended Trump's authority to restart strikes without Congress.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump warns Iran: decimation will be continued as House War Powers vote fails 212-212
outlets.summary: CNN covers Trump's Truth Social threat of resumed Iran strikes and the House's third failed War Powers vote (212-212 tie), while also reporting Lebanon-Israel ceasefire extended 45 days.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Tensions flare near Strait of Hormuz as a ship is seized and another is sunk
outlets.summary: NPR reports on two maritime incidents: the IRGC seizure of the Hui Chan floating armory near Fujairah and the earlier sinking of Indian cargo ship MSV Haji Ali off Oman.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump says Xi offered to help end Iran conflict; China says Strait 'must remain open'
outlets.summary: Fox News covers the Trump-Xi Beijing summit, framing Xi's offer of diplomatic help as a diplomatic win for Trump. Fox highlights Trump's 'patience running out' warnings to Iran and his willingness to accept a 20-year nuclear suspension. China agrees Hormuz must reopen.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump vows decimation of Iran to be continued as ceasefire violations mount
outlets.summary: Fox News covers Trump's Truth Social post and Hannity interview promising resumed Iran strikes. Also covers Iran's ceasefire violation pattern (10+ attacks on US forces, 9 on commercial ships) that Fox describes as still "below the threshold" for restarting major combat.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: LIVE UPDATES: Trump Declares Iran 'Under Control' As He Vows To 'Finish The Job'
outlets.summary: Daily Wire coverage emphasizes Trump's confidence that Iran is 'under control,' his vow to 'finish the job,' and ongoing protests inside Iran demanding an end to Supreme Leader rule. Covers IRGC's 5-day war exercise near Tehran as evidence of Iran's continued aggression.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live: Lebanon ceasefire extended, Israel launches deadly strikes; Araghchi doubts US seriousness
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera's May 16 liveblog covers: Lebanon-Israel 45-day ceasefire extension; Araghchi stating Iran doubts US seriousness and nuclear issue is in deadlock; Trump-Xi summit yielding no binding Iran deal; and ongoing Israeli strikes in Lebanon despite extension.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war Day 77: BRICS collapses without joint statement, ceasefire extended, IRGC seizes vessel
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with the BRICS summit collapse (first ever without joint statement), Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension, and IRGC seizure of the Hui Chan near Fujairah.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to 45-day extension of ceasefire, U.S. State Department says
outlets.summary: PBS reports on the 45-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension following the third round of US-hosted Washington talks, and separately covers the Hormuz ship seizure.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Report: Israel, US readying for renewed strikes on Iran for as early as next week
outlets.summary: Times of Israel (citing NYT) reports US and Israel in 'most intense strike preparation since ceasefire' — options include commando mission to extract nuclear material from Iranian sites and seizure of Kharg Island. Readiness targeted for week of May 18, one day after ceasefire expires May 17.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Netanyahu: 'I promised we would change the face of the Middle East — that is exactly what we are doing'
outlets.summary: Times of Israel liveblog: Netanyahu's pre-Shabbat video on 45-day Lebanon ceasefire extension. Separately: Israeli officials cite Hamas Gaza military chief Haddad strike as targeting someone 'undermining Trump's Gaza plan.' Israel remains on alert for ceasefire collapse with Iran as May 17 expiry approaches.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Israel strikes Hamas Gaza chief Haddad with 13 bombs; ceasefire extended 45 days; IDF kills 15 Hezbollah
outlets.summary: Times of Israel liveblog covers the IAF strike on Hamas leader Haddad in Gaza, the Lebanon ceasefire extension, IDF killing of 15 Hezbollah operatives in 40+ airstrikes, and the death of Sgt. Negev Dagan.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IRGC launches 5-day war exercise near Tehran simulating US invasion; CIA says Iran can outlast blockade
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post covers the IRGC 'Martyred Leader' 5-day exercise near Tehran simulating a US ground invasion. Also covers the CIA/DIA assessment that Iran has retained 70% of its pre-war missile arsenal and can withstand the blockade for months — directly contradicting Trump and Hegseth's public statements to Congress.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi: US must lift blockade before Hormuz talks; Iran will not bow to coercion
outlets.summary: PressTV covers Araghchi's latest statements dubbing the ceasefire 'very shaky' and insisting the US naval blockade must end before Iran discusses Hormuz reopening. Iranian state media emphasizes national resilience and frames negotiations as Iran not surrendering but protecting its rights.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi at BRICS: US bullying must end; Iran rallies Global South against aggression
outlets.summary: Press TV frames Araghchi's BRICS speech as a diplomatic victory, emphasizing his call to unite against US bullying. Iranian state media downplays the BRICS collapse without joint statement.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian privately called IRGC UAE attacks 'madness'; publicly praises national unity
outlets.summary: Iran International reports the widening gap between Pezeshkian's public unity message and his reported private fury: he allegedly called IRGC attacks on UAE 'completely irresponsible and madness' and sought urgent meeting with Mojtaba Khamenei to halt them. Separately, Pezeshkian issued national unity video praising the nation's 'minimal' economic fluctuations during the war.
